name: Maryna Viazovska
reason: for solving the sphere-packing problem in dimensions 8 and 24

name: Hugo Duminil-Copin
reason: for solving long-standing problems in the probabilistic theory of phase transitions in statistical physics

name: June Huh
reason: for bringing the ideas of Hodge theory to combinatorics

name: James Maynard
reason: for contributions to analytic number theory that have led to major advances in the understanding of prime number distribution

name: Peter Scholze
reason: for transforming arithmetic algebraic geometry through his introduction of perfectoid spaces

name: Caucher Birkar
reason: for proving the boundedness of Fano varieties and contributions to the minimal model program

name: Maryam Mirzakhani
reason: for outstanding contributions to the dynamics and geometry of Riemann surfaces and their moduli spaces

name: Terence Tao
reason: for his contributions to partial differential equations, combinatorics, harmonic analysis and additive number theory

name: Cédric Villani
reason: for his proofs of nonlinear Landau damping and convergence to equilibrium for the Boltzmann equation

name: Shing-Tung Yau
reason: for his contributions to differential geometry and partial differential equations, including the proof of the Calabi conjecture

name: John Milnor
reason: for his work in differential topology, including the discovery of exotic spheres

name: Lars Ahlfors
reason: for his contributions to complex analysis, including the theory of Riemann surfaces
